S

Unibox — Mobile app

Flow document · v1 en-AU

How the native Unibox behaves on the phone — every screen, what happens when you tap each action on a card, how each add-on shows up, and the mechanics underneath. Built to mirror pages/unibox-mobile.html. Screenshots are from the live prototype.

What Unibox Mobile is

Unibox is the app's action centre — the answer to "if I've got 5 minutes, what do I actually need to do today?" On the phone it's a calm, focused, one-thing-at-a-time flow. Same product and the same data as the web Unibox, tuned for a thumb.

The idea in one line

Everything that needs the business owner — an overdue invoice, an unmatched bank line, an expense missing a receipt — is gathered into one place, ranked by urgency, and made actionable right there. Clear it, feel progress, move on.

Mobile ≠ a cut-down web app

Web is where serious work happens; mobile complements it. But a mobile-only owner can still do everything from the phone — send, follow up, match, add a receipt, record an asset, convert a quote. Simple, but complete.

How you get in and what you see

Enter
Dashboard
A calm banner "N things need your attention" (+ 3 other entry points) opens Unibox.
→
Focus
Do these first
A swipe deck of the top-5 most-urgent items, one card at a time.
→
Act
Tap a CTA
Most actions open a bottom sheet (Match, Follow-up, Add receipt…).
→
Done
Confetti + Undo
The item clears, health rises, a 5-second Undo is one tap away.
Three things the team flagged, decided here:
  • Expenses now use "Match" + "Add receipt". On each expense row, the old Categorise is replaced by Match and a second, always-visible Add receipt button. "Match" opens the app's Match sheet (adapted for an expense). See Cards & actions → Expenses.
  • The add-ons section is a forward-looking spec. The mobile prototype ships the core modules today; the per-add-on cards below describe how each would behave on mobile, taken from the web model. See Add-ons.
  • This change is mobile-only for now (the feedback was about the mobile app); web parity is an easy follow-up.

Screens & navigation

There are three surfaces: the Dashboard (the app home), the Unibox screen (full-screen, the tab bar hides), and the More drawer. Bottom sheets slide up over any of them.

1 · Entry points — four ways into Unibox

From the Dashboard you can open Unibox from a prominent banner, from either of the two red/amber stat cards, from the header bell, or from the top row of the More drawer. Each carries the live "needs you" count.

DashboardBanner "17 things need your attention", the Overdue / Unmatched stat cards, and the header bell (badge 17) all open Unibox.
More drawerUnibox is pinned to the top with a count badge ("Your action centre"). The tab bar's "More" tab opens this.
When the list is empty the banner flips to "You're all caught up ✨ — Nothing needs you right now." (green).

2 · The Unibox screen (top → bottom)

Opening Unibox is a full-screen push — the bottom tab bar hides so nothing competes with the task; a back arrow returns you to the Dashboard.

Top
Header + mini ring
Back arrow · "Unibox" · a health ring that appears top-right once you scroll past the health card, filling live as you clear items.
→
Anchor
Business Health
Score ring, band ("A few things to tidy"), streak, and an ⓘ that explains the score.
→
Focus
Do these first
The swipe deck (top-5) + a "N to action" counter.
→
Rest
Everything else
Collapsed behind "Show everything else · N cards"; opens to the category cards.
Unibox — topBusiness Health card + "Do these first" swipe deck. Front card is the most urgent (a 14-day overdue invoice).
Health ⓘ explainer"How your score works" + "What the streak means" — tap the ⓘ to open/close.
Everything elseCategory cards (Get paid, Bank feed, Expenses, Assets) + Liabilities & equity + Key dates. Each shows the first 3 rows with "Show N more".
When you clear the whole list, the deck and grid are replaced by a single celebration card: "You're all clear ✨ — Everything's actioned. Your business is in the green."

3 · Bottom sheets

Actions that need a choice slide up as bottom sheets over a dimmed scrim; tap the scrim or Cancel to dismiss. They always sit above the swipe deck. Full detail in Action sheets.

The swipe deck — "Do these first"

Instead of a long list, the top priorities are a Tinder-style deck: one big card at a time, the rest stacked behind. It keeps attention on a single decision.

How it behaves

  • One card, shown big: type tag, title, amount, a status pill (e.g. "14 days overdue"), and for bank items an ✨ AI-match hint ("Pays INV-1038 · 98%").
  • Swipe to browse. Swiping the front card sends it below the stack and the next rises — it does not resolve anything, so you can't dismiss an invoice by accident.
  • Dots deplete. A row of dots shows how many are in this batch; as you resolve items the dots shrink (5 → 4 → …), then the next 5 most-urgent load.
  • Act from the CTAs below the card, not by swiping.

The CTAs under the card

A big primary button runs the card's main action, with the secondary options shown as ghost buttons beside/below it (destructive ones — "Not a match" — read in red).

Overdue invoice → Follow up Mark paid Snooze
Bank line → Match Not a match
Expense → Match Add receipt

The deck reads from the same ranked list as the cards below, so the same item never contradicts itself.

Completing from the deck fires confetti + a snackbar Undo and advances to the next card; the health ring and "N to action" update instantly.

Cards & their actions

Under "Everything else" the work is grouped into cards. Every row has a primary button; most also have secondary actions. Here's exactly what each tap does.

Get paid Invoices & quotes

RowPrimary tapSecondary
Draft invoice (to send)Send → sends it, item clears + confetti + UndoPreview → opens preview (no dismiss)
Overdue invoiceFollow up → opens the Follow-up email sheetMark paid · Snooze → both clear the item
Due soonSend reminder → clears + confetti + UndoSnooze
Accepted quoteConvert → converts to invoice, clears + UndoView → preview only

Bank feed Reconcile

RowPrimary tapSecondary
Unmatched, confident AI matchMatch → Match sheet with Sole's suggestion pre-selected + a confidence %Not a match → leaves it for you
Unmatched, no confident matchCategorise → Categorise sheet (pick a category)Ignore → clears it

Expenses Updated

This is the change the team asked for. Every expense row now shows two always-visible buttons — Match and Add receipt — replacing the old single "Categorise" + hidden overflow. So you can reconcile the expense and attach its receipt without opening a menu.
Expenses card (updated)Each row: Match + Add receipt. No more ⋯ — both actions are on the row.
TapWhat happens
MatchOpens the Match sheet titled "Match this expense" — link the expense to the bank payment. Shows the expense line + candidate payments; Confirm match clears it (+ confetti + Undo). (Reuses the app's Match machinery; "Match" is Sole's standard reconcile verb.)
Add receiptOpens the Add receipt sheet — "Take a photo" or "Choose a file". Either attaches the receipt and clears the item (+ confetti + Undo).

Assets Tidy up

RowPrimary tapSecondary
Possible asset (purchase)Record → Record asset sheetNot an asset → clears it
No payment linkedMatch → Match sheet (link the payment)Later → clears it

Liabilities & equity · Key dates Informational

CardRowsAction
Liabilities & equityGST to set aside, equipment loanSet aside · Schedule (demo confirmation; these are reference rows, not to-dos)
Key dates AU taxBAS Q4, Super guarantee, Income tax returnDue-date pill only (read-only)

Action sheets

Actions that need a choice open a bottom sheet. Every "confirm" button clears the item optimistically, fires confetti, and shows a 5-second Undo.

Match · expense"Match this expense" — link it to the bank payment. Choose a candidate → Confirm match.
Follow-up emailPre-filled, editable reminder to the client. Send email clears the invoice.
CategorisePick a category chip → Save category. (Bank-feed lines with no confident match.)
Add receipt"Take a photo" or "Choose a file" — either attaches and clears the item.
Record assetExplains depreciation, then Record asset adds it to the register.
⋯ OverflowOn rows with more than one action, the ⋯ lists them all; picking one runs it.
SheetOpened byConfirm button → result
Match (transaction)Bank feed Match, Asset MatchConfirm match (disabled until a candidate is picked) → "Matched … reconciled"
Match (expense)Expense MatchConfirm match → "Matched {name} to a payment"
Follow-up emailOverdue invoice Follow upSend email → "Follow-up sent to {client}"
CategoriseBank line CategoriseSave category (disabled until chosen) → "{name} categorised as {category}"
Add receiptExpense Add receipt"Take a photo" / "Choose a file" → "Receipt attached to {name}"
Record assetAsset RecordRecord asset → "{name} recorded as an asset"
⋯ OverflowThe ⋯ on any multi-action rowLists every action; picking re-runs it through the same paths above

Add-ons — how each one behaves

Forward-looking spec. The mobile prototype today ships the core modules (Get paid, Bank feed, Expenses, Assets, Liabilities, Key dates). This section describes how each add-on would surface on mobile, taken from the web app's model, so the team can build them consistently. The pattern is always the same: an add-on unlocks a module → that module adds one card under "Everything else", and its urgent items join the swipe deck. Rows use the same buttons + bottom-sheet grammar shown in Cards & actions.

The rule (same as web)

A business is on one plan and can switch on add-ons. A card appears only when its module is unlocked — by the plan or an enabled add-on. On mobile the gating works identically; today the prototype simply hardcodes the core set.

PlanPriceModules included
Free$0Invoicing (sales), Expenses, basic Assets
Business$14.99+ Quotes
Compliance Bundle 25% OFF$24.99+ Accounting & any 2 add-ons free
Advanced BEST VALUE$44.99+ Accounting, Time, Projects, Advanced Assets, KM, Booking
Pro$59.99Coming June 2026

Each add-on on mobile

Add-onMobile card it addsWhat's on itRow actions → behaviour
Accounting $7.99Bank feed + Liabilities & equityUnmatched transactions; GST to set aside, loansMatch → match sheet · Categorise → category sheet · Set aside / Schedule → confirm
Time Tracker $4.99Time trackerUnbilled hours ready to invoiceInvoice → creates invoice + clears · Log / Stop timer → clears
Projects $4.99ProjectsOver-budget jobs, milestones dueReview → confirm sheet → Mark done · View → info (no dismiss)
Advanced Assets $4.99Assets (richer)Possible assets, depreciation promptsRecord → record-asset sheet · Match → match a payment
KM Tracker $4.99KM trackerTrips to classify (business vs personal)Classify → clears · Log trip → clears
Booking $4.99Work coming upUpcoming bookings + $ booked (forecast)Invoice / Send quote → clears
Inventory $4.99InventoryLow-stock itemsReorder / Count → clears
Investment $4.99InvestmentsDividends to record, CGT eventsReview → confirm sheet · View → info
Crypto SOONCrypto (teaser)A calm "coming soon" cardNone — teaser only, never charged
Action verbs behave predictably: do-it verbs (Send, Invoice, Log, Classify, Reorder, Count, Record, Set aside, Schedule, Remind me…) clear the item with confetti + Undo; "Review" opens a confirm sheet before clearing; "View / Details" opens an info sheet and dismisses nothing (looking at something shouldn't delete it).

Mechanics

The small set of rules that make the whole thing feel alive.

Business Health score

Think of it like a tidiness score for the books.

  • Starts at 100.
  • Things that need you pull it down — an overdue invoice more than a small expense.
  • Clearing them lifts it back up, live.
  • It never hits zero — even a messy day stays encouraging.

80–100 In the green 60–79 A few things to tidy below 60 Needs attention

Streak

Days in a row you've checked in and cleared at least one thing. It never counts down — a quiet day just doesn't add to it. It's there to keep the habit, gently.

Live rings

The health ring in the card, and the mini ring that appears in the header once you scroll, share the same score — so both fill in real time as you clear items.

Completing an item

Tap
Confirm an action
e.g. Send email, Confirm match, Save category.
→
Instant
Item clears
Removed optimistically; count, dots and rings update at once.
→
Reward
Confetti
A small burst; a bigger one for milestones (3 · 5 · 10 done, a tier-up, or all-clear).
→
Safety
Undo (5s)
A snackbar with Undo restores the item exactly where it was.
Complete → confetti + UndoHealth rose 60→67, "16 to action · 1 done", streak +1, and the snackbar offers Undo.
Motion respects the OS Reduce Motion setting — confetti and slide animations are skipped for anyone who's turned it on.
